🕵 Manual Reproduction

✓ Real evidence was recovered — follow the steps below.

Step 1 — WAV Extracted
Evidence: Carved at offset 0x00000000, 60% confidence
Why: Valid RIFF/WAVE, size=617,436 bytes
⬇ Download 00000000.wav
Result: See 00000000.wav below.
Step 2 — Run Strings
Evidence: 20 printable string(s) extracted
Why: A flag or hint may appear directly as printable text.
Result: Nothing useful.

Final Conclusion

No hidden flag found from the steps above.

Additional Investigation Tips

🔍 Embedded file signatures: JPEG, AAC_ADTS_MPEG2, AAC_ADTS_MPEG4, MP3_FRAME.
  → Run: binwalk -e <file>  to extract.
🔑 steghide found nothing with common passphrases.
  → Try: stegseek <file> /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
  → Or build a custom wordlist from metadata (artist/title/year).
📂 1 file(s) extracted — analyse each:
  → file <extracted_file>
  → Check for nested steganography in extracted images.
🔤 XOR single-byte brute-force hit — review cipher_analysis.txt.
🖼 Review spectrogram.png:
  → Look for hidden text/images at 15–22 kHz
  → Use Sonic Visualiser for interactive zoomed inspection
  → Compare left vs right channel for differences
📊 Review fft.png:
  → Unusual spikes at specific frequencies may encode data
  → Dead-silent frequency bands may be used for covert channels

⚠ Warnings (2)

⚠ qsstv is installed but is a GUI-only application with no batch/CLI decode mode — cannot be used in this headless pipeline
⚠ multimon-ng is installed but has no SSTV demodulator — VIS detection is performed by this tool's own Goertzel-based analysis instead
